← all tutorials

Architectural Patterns — interactive ARCHITECTURAL_PATTERNS.md architectural_patterns.py

How you structure a system: the deployment spectrum (monolith → SOA → microservices → serverless), the service mesh / sidecar, event-driven fan-out, and CQRS. Pick a topology to see its profile, drag service count to watch the mesh overhead grow, then open the tradeoff matrix and adjust the weight profile — the winner flips live. All scores are recomputed in JS from the identical model as architectural_patterns.py.

① Topology comparison — monolith / SOA / microservices / serverless

What to do: click a topology. Each is scored 1–5 on six axes (5 = best). The monolith wins dev-velocity & latency; microservices win scalability & fault-isolation; serverless wins ops-simple + scale. The score bars are the consensus tradeoffs — no topology dominates every axis.
The database boundary IS the service boundary: sharing a DB makes a "distributed monolith" with all microservice costs and none of the benefits. Distribution adds a latency tax (~1µs in-process → ~1ms gRPC → ~500ms cold start).

② Service mesh & sidecar — the O(N) data plane tax

What to do: drag services N. Each pod runs a sidecar proxy (~64MB Envoy) in the data plane (intercepts every call); the control plane (Istiod) pushes routes/certs out-of-band. The logical call graph can be O(N²) but the data plane stays O(N). Worth it past ~20 services; pure overhead below ~10.
sidecar = 64 MB
sidecar RAM (linear) logical links O(N²) ~20 services = break-even

③ Event-driven fan-out & CQRS — temporal decoupling vs read lag

What to do: drag fan-out K — one event fans out to K independent consumers (each in its own consumer group; the broker buffers the burst). Then toggle the CQRS read-lag slider: a CDC stream keeps the denormalized read model 50–500ms behind the normalized write model. Fine for a feed; unacceptable for "read your own writes".
1 event → 4 consumers (parallelism = partitions)
Redis ~100K QPS at $500/mo >> a 4th read replica ~5K QPS at $5000/mo — cache first, replicate second. CQRS pushes this further: a purpose-built read store per query shape.

④ Gold check — the tradeoff matrix, winner recomputed live

What this proves: the page re-runs the weighted scoring on the identical axis table as architectural_patterns.py GOLD CHECK. The green check: OK below confirms the JS scores + per-profile winners match the .py exactly.
check: … JS weighted scores + per-profile winners match architectural_patterns.py GOLD CHECK (MVP→monolith=44, hyperscale→microservices=49).
